title: ACHR vs JOBY Joby Aviation eVTOL Competition Comparison
text: Archer Aviation vs Joby Aviation and Vertical Aerospace comparison: Archer aims primarily to be eVTOL original equipment manufacturer (OEM), while Joby intends to be vertically integrated transportation-as-a-service (TaaS) company. Archer outsources manufacturing via Stellantis, prioritizing defense and UAE entry, while Joby's vertical integration supports Dubai commercialization with Uber and revenue from partnerships ($53 million in 2025). Regulatory progress: Archer completed third stage becoming first US eVTOL company to do so, while Joby flown first FAA-conforming aircraft for Type Inspection Authorization. Archer became first eVTOL player to secure FAA acceptance for 100% of Means of Compliance (MOC), key step in certification process. Joby stuck around 97% on this step. Financial position: Joby ended quarter with $2.5 billion in cash and short-term investments, stronger than Archer. Archer's balance sheet remains key strength with around $2 billion cash and order backlog exceeding $6 billion. Vertical Aerospace reported completed world's first piloted wing-borne flight transition by full-scale vectored-thrust eVTOL aircraft in April. Analyst sentiment: ACHR and EVTL both carry Moderate Buy ratings, JOBY holds Hold consensus. EVTL offers highest upside potential 371%. Average ACHR price target $12.33, implying 89% upside from current levels.
